vehicle.label: 6-DOF Industrial Arm
vehicle.desc: 6-DOF serial industrial robot arm, 5kg payload, 600mm reach

parts.label: Base Joint (J1)
parts.spec: Base rotary joint J1, +-180deg, harmonic drive, 100Nm peak torque, mounting flange ISO 9409

parts.label: Shoulder Joint (J2)
parts.spec: Shoulder pitch joint J2, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 80Nm, cable routing through center bore

parts.label: Upper Arm (Link 2-3)
parts.spec: Upper arm link, 300mm length, hollow tube with cable conduit, lightweight Al 7075

parts.label: Elbow Joint (J3)
parts.spec: Elbow pitch joint J3, +-150deg, cycloidal reducer, 50Nm, encoder dual-stack

parts.label: Forearm + Wrist (J4)
parts.spec: Forearm with wrist roll J4, 250mm length, +-360deg continuous, slip ring for power/signal

parts.label: Wrist Pitch (J5)
parts.spec: Wrist pitch joint J5, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 20Nm, compact form factor

parts.label: Wrist Roll (J6)
parts.spec: Wrist final roll joint J6, +-360deg, harmonic drive, 10Nm, ISO 9409-1 50mm tool flange

parts.label: Tool Flange + Changer
parts.spec: Tool changer master plate at J6 end, pneumatic locking, 4 utility ports

parts.label: Cable Management Spine
parts.spec: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic

parts.label: Replaceable Motor Module Set
parts.spec: Actuation_motors closure module: replaceable motor_module cartridges for J1-J6, fastener_access, coupling_dummy, insert/washer preload pads, torque_Nm intent labels

parts.label: Encoder Cover + Sensing Set
parts.spec: Sensing_encoders closure module: encoder_pocket and encoder_cover at each joint, index marks, cable exit strain relief, inspection labels

parts.label: Drive Electronics Bay
parts.spec: Drive_electronics bay with driver_bay, power_channel, signal_channel, ground_point, service cover, connector keying, thermal inspection window

requirements: Shoulder pitch joint J2, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 80Nm, cable routing through center bore
Vehicle context: 6-DOF serial industrial robot arm, 5kg payload, 600mm reach
Material: Al 7075 + steel + composite, process: CNC + LPBF

verify.rule: Ensure all necessary fields are included in the blueprint.
The estimated mass does not exceed the specified budget.

verify.value: All required features are present in the geometry.
verify.rule: Each required feature must be represented in the geometry operations.

verify.value: Interfaces are reciprocal and named correctly.
verify.rule: Adjacent interfaces must be named and connected reciprocally.

verify.value: Fastener locking methods and preload evidence are provided.
verify.rule: Critical joints must state locking method and preload/torque intent.

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to mixed materials (Al 7075, steel, composite).
risk.mit: Use thermal interface materials and conductive paths to manage heat.

risk.desc: Risk of harmonic drive failure under peak torque conditions.
risk.mit: Implement over-torque protection mechanisms and regular maintenance checks.

risk.desc: Cable management could become complex with multiple entry points.
risk.mit: Optimize cable routing and ensure proper strain relief at all exits.

risk.desc: Structural integrity concerns if fasteners are not properly preloaded.
risk.mit: Specify torque values and ensure all fasteners are tightened accordingly.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to tolerance stack-up.
risk.mit: Implement alignment aids and conduct thorough quality checks post-assembly.

physics_paths.description: Rotary motion between base plate and mounting flange
Torque load from payload to harmonic drive
Heat dissipation from base plate to ambient air

risk.desc: Mass exceeds initial estimate, may affect overall arm dynamics.
risk.mit: Review material selection and manufacturing process for weight optimization.

risk.desc: Potential for cable interference during full range of motion.
risk.mit: Implement dynamic simulation to ensure cable clearance throughout motion.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal expansion affecting joint alignment over time.
risk.mit: Include thermal expansion joints and regular maintenance checks.

risk.desc: Complexity in assembly due to multiple interfaces and features.
risk.mit: Develop detailed assembly instructions and training materials.

risk.desc: Potential for misalignment if datum visibility is compromised.
risk.mit: Ensure primary datum faces are clearly visible and labeled.

manufacturing_strategy.support: Horizontal orientation for machining, vertical for LPBF

risk.desc: Potential failure in torque tests due to insufficient fastener locking methods.
risk.mit: Specify locking methods and torque/preload values for critical joints.

risk.desc: Risk of cable damage during wire pull tests due to inadequate routing.
risk.mit: Ensure cable routing includes slack loops and strain relief at all joints.

risk.desc: Insufficient visibility for inspection of internal components.
risk.mit: Increase size of inspection windows and add more visual indicators.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment issues due to loose tolerances.
risk.mit: Review and tighten manufacturing tolerances to IT7 or higher.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal stress due to poor heat dissipation paths.
risk.mit: Optimize thermal paths to ensure efficient heat dissipation.

generated_parts.label: Cable Management Spine
generated_parts.spec: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic

requirements: External cable carrier from base to wrist
Drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic
Cable spine with slack loops at every joint
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry
Connectors have finger/tool clearance and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Joints show a bearing seat with shoulder or retaining feature
Harness crossing each joint has slack loop, strain relief at both ends, and routing that avoids pinch points
Every inter-part interface names a specific connection type with its DOF and a numeric clearance
Parts can be installed in a stated order where every fastener, connector, and mating feature stays reachable
One primary datum part anchors the assembly; each other part locates off a named parent datum
Every part's primary load traces through named interfaces to the base/ground
The assembly uses the fewest interfaces that still allow service and manufacture

verify.rule: All required evidence_features present in part_tree/geometry_ops.
Mass estimate <= 200g.
Each part_tree child and geometry_op is traceable by id/name.
Adjacent interfaces named with exact neighboring subsystem labels and correct connection_type + DOF + clearance_mm + required_feature.
Critical joints show visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence.

risk.desc: Potential interference between cable spine and shoulder module.
risk.mit: Review clearance and adjust geometry if necessary.

risk.desc: Insufficient strain relief at wrist joint may cause cable damage.
risk.mit: Add additional strain relief geometry near wrist joint.

risk.desc: Service channel may be too narrow for some maintenance tools.
risk.mit: Increase width of service channel to accommodate standard tools.

risk.desc: Bolted interface at base may require additional washers for preload.
risk.mit: Specify washers with appropriate preload for bolted interface.

risk.desc: Snap fit interface at shoulder may loosen over time due to vibrations.
risk.mit: Consider alternative connection type or add locking mechanism.

generated_parts.label: Drive Electronics Bay
generated_parts.spec: Drive_electronics bay with driver_bay, power_channel, signal_channel, ground_point, service cover, connector keying, thermal inspection window

requirements: Drive Electronics Bay with driver bay, power channel, signal channel, ground point, service cover, connector keying, thermal inspection window
Driver board bay, power/signal separation along the arm
Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles, with the separation visible in routing geometry

PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings, and mounting holes match standard board patterns
